title: Ardleigh II Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed including pilot Flt Sgt. Charles Henry Bray

external_narratives.summary: On the evening of 25 February 1943, eleven Halifax II bombers of 102 (Ceylon) Squadron took off from RAF Pocklington for a raid on Nuremberg. DT800 (coded DY-P) crashed at Ardleigh at 20:24, about an hour after takeoff, likely due to icing causing loss of control.
key_details: Crashed at 20:24, one hour after takeoff
Carrying 1000lb bomb, incendiaries and propaganda leaflets
Explosion created crater 30 yards diameter, 8ft deep

external_narratives.summary: The Wartime Memories Project documents RAF Pocklington as the base of 102 (Ceylon) Squadron, from which Halifax DT800 departed on its final flight.
Local press coverage of the memorial unveiling for the crew of Halifax DT800, honouring the eight crew members killed when the bomber crashed at Ardleigh during a Nuremberg raid.
